summary: Verify existing test suite passes as baseline before changes; Add test 'payload type field does not override event discriminant' to claudeBridge.test.ts. Send a text_delta event where payload contains `{ type: 'EVIL', id: 'msg-1', content: 'Hello' }`. Assert handler receives `{ type: 'text_delta', id: 'msg-1', content: 'Hello' }` — the hardcoded discriminant must win. This test FAILS with the current spread implementation because `{ type: 'text_delta', ...{type:'EVIL'} }` evaluates to `{type:'EVIL'}`.; Replace spread-cast pattern in subscribeToClaudeEvents (bridge.ts:158-166) with explicit field extraction for all 7 event types. Keep the EventEntry array and registration loop but change each handler to extract only expected fields from `event.payload` (e.g. `const p = e.payload; handler({ type: 'text_delta', id: p.id as string, content: p.content as string })`) instead of spreading. The `as string`/`as Record<...>` casts are equivalent safety to the prior `as OutboundMessage` — we trust the Tauri contract for field shapes but construct the discriminated union ourselves. The 'claude-ready' handler remains unchanged (no payload). All existing claudeBridge.test.ts tests must continue to pass.; Add test 'logs error to console when subscribeToClaudeEvents fails' in App.test.tsx. Mock `subscribeToClaudeEvents` to reject with `new Error('subscribe failed')`, spy on `console.error`, render App, wait for ready phase. Assert `console.error` was called with arguments matching `'[claude] subscribeToClaudeEvents failed:'` and the error. Also assert the existing toast ('Claude assistant unavailable') still appears. This FAILS because current code uses `catch (_err)` without calling console.error.; In App.tsx initApp(), change the subscribeToClaudeEvents catch block (line 266-268) from `catch (_err) { showToast(...) }` to `catch (err) { console.error('[claude] subscribeToClaudeEvents failed:', err); showToast('Claude assistant unavailable — chat features may not work', 'error'); }`.; Add test 'shows toast when claudeAbort fails' in App.test.tsx. Mock `claudeAbort` to reject with `new Error('abort failed')`. Render App, wait for ready. Simulate the abort flow: capture the `subscribeToClaudeEvents` handler callback, fire a `text_delta` event to put claudeStore into 'responding' state, then find and click the AbortButton. Assert a toast containing 'abort' and the error message appears, and console.error was called. This FAILS because current `onAbort` only calls `console.error` with no toast.; In App.tsx, change the onAbort handler (line 74-76) from `claudeAbort().catch(console.error)` to `claudeAbort().catch((err) => { console.error('[claude] abort failed:', err); showToast(`Abort failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`, 'error'); })`.; Add compile-time type assertions to types.typecheck.ts: import ClaudeMessageContext from bridge and MessageContext from claudeStore, define `type _Expected = Pick<MessageContext, 'selectedEntity' | 'diagnostics' | 'constraints'>`, assert bidirectional assignability (`_Expected assignable to ClaudeMessageContext` and vice versa). This serves as a guard that both types stay in structural sync. Verify tsc --noEmit passes.

Replaced standalone ClaudeMessageContext interface with Pick<MessageContext, ...> in bridge.ts. Fixed duplicate import of MessageContext that was causing test failures. All 18 claudeBridge tests pass, no type errors in our files.

In bridge.ts, replace the standalone `ClaudeMessageContext` interface (lines 114-118) with `export type ClaudeMessageContext = Pick<MessageContext, 'selectedEntity' | 'diagnostics' | 'constraints'>`. Add `import type { MessageContext } from './stores/claudeStore'`. This makes the bridge↔store type coupling explicit. No circular dependency: bridge→claudeStore is a type-only import; claudeStore does not import from bridge. All existing tests and the new type assertions must pass.

Added toast DOM assertion to claudeAbort failure test. The test now verifies both console.error and the toast element (type=error, content contains Abort failed), matching the pattern used by all other error-path tests. All 95 tests pass. Plan complete.

Fix misleading test assertion in 'shows toast when claudeAbort fails' (App.test.tsx ~line 1978). The test title promises toast verification but only asserts console.error. After the existing consoleSpy assertion (line 2009-2012), add a second waitFor block that asserts the toast DOM element: `await waitFor(() => { const toastEl = screen.getByTestId('toast'); expect(toastEl.dataset.type).toBe('error'); expect(toastEl.textContent).toContain('Abort failed: abort failed'); });`. This aligns the test with every other error-path test in the file that verifies the toast via screen.getByTestId('toast'). No impl change needed — step-6 already emits the toast; this step only fixes the incomplete test assertion.

Step-10 (rebase onto origin/main): Branch task/522 was already based on latest origin/main (merge-base == origin/main HEAD). No rebase needed. All 95 tests pass (18 bridge + 77 App). No TS errors in task files (4 pre-existing errors in claudeIntegration.test.ts from another task). Branch pushed to origin/task/522. All 10 plan steps complete.

Rebase task branch onto latest origin/main to resolve fast-forward merge failure. Fetch origin, rebase onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts in gui/src/App.tsx or gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (the files most likely to have diverged from concurrent tasks). Run vitest on the 3 test files to verify no regressions after rebase. Force-push the rebased branch.

Post-escalation verification: all 10 plan steps already done, 95 tests passing (18 bridge + 77 App). Escalation handler triaged 14 review suggestions into 3 follow-up tasks (#546, #547, #548). No changes needed to this task. Plan complete.

Step-11 (rebase for merge retry): origin/main has been restructured — all 3 target bugs eliminated. subscribeToClaudeEvents removed from bridge.ts, ClaudeMessageContext removed, claudeAbort now synchronous in claudeStore.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts never existed on main. Cherry-pick of 10 task commits fails with modify/delete conflicts. Branch has 469 commits ahead of main (459 task-mgmt auto-commits + 10 task commits). Escalated as blocker (esc-522-20) recommending task cancellation — fixes are obsolete.

Step-11 marked done — escalation esc-522-20 was resolved by steward confirming all 3 target bugs are eliminated on origin/main via structural refactoring (subscribeToClaudeEvents removed, ClaudeMessageContext removed, claudeAbort now synchronous). No code from this branch needs to be merged. Rebase/merge retry is moot. All 11 steps complete.
